Activity related to the oversight of

Medicare and Medicaid Services
(CMS). Health Plan Operation (HPO)
Support and Finance participate in

series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership, supervisory, managerial, executive, administrative, substantive, and other high- level correspondence in any media, including electronic communication, pertaining to the formulation, planning, implementation, interpretation, modification, or redefinition of the programs, services, or projects of an agency and the administrative regulations, policies, and procedures that govern them. ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
AIN 5004 should be used only for
correspondence that is not included
in or directly related to another
records series on the agency's
approved schedule. For example, a
memorandum that documents an
appropriations request must be
retained for the minimum retention
period prescribed by AIN 5568; a
letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by AIN 5195, etc. See
also AIN 5006.

series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, re: routine matters, general inquiries, and requests for materials. series_title: Directives: Routine Issuances, General Administrative & Office Procedures

executive staff, board or

General.

Library and Archives Commission.

series_description: Self evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans With Disabilities Act.

Records of temporary usefulness that are not an integral part of a records series of an agency, that are not regularly filed within an agency’s recordkeeping system, and that are required only for a limited period of time for the completion of an action by an official or employee of the agency or in the preparation of an ongoing records series. Transitory records are not essential to the fulfillment of statutory obligations or to the documentation of agency functions. Some examples of transitory information, which can be in any medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy, etc.) are routine messages; telephone message notifications; internal meeting notices; routing slips; incoming letters or memoranda of transmittal that add nothing of substance to enclosures; and similar routine information used for communication, but not for the documentation, of a specific agency transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part of
a records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
records disposition logs (AIN 5211),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (AIN 5212).

series_title: Plans of Correction and Evidence Books
series_description: Plans of correction and evidence books are utilized by facilities to prove that cited deficiencies from regulatory services, we addressed and that other individuals who may be affected from the deficient practice were identified and addressed to ensure the deficient practice is unlikely to occur. Evidence can be a wide variety of items, depending on the citation. This can include IDT meetings, committee meeting minutes, fire drill forms, environmental checks, training rosters, etc. The POC books and evidence are needed until the citation can be cleared by regulatory services. Because there is no ACC (automatic cancelation clause) as in years past, deficiencies may remain open for longer periods of time (as much as a year from annual to annual).

series_title: LOS Cards, Home Shift and Hygiene Logs
series_description: LOS Cards are records showing the level of supervision (how closely a client needs to be supervised by staff for safety) each individual. Home Shift Log are kept every day on each home. They are a general record of events for the day for both clients and staff. They contain PHI for multiple residents. They may be used to review who was present during a shift or what events and incidents occurred that day. One of the common uses is for internal incident investigations. Hygiene logs are sometimes used to document when clients had their teeth brushed, nails clipped, took a shower, etc. It may be reviewed when there is a concern related to hygiene (e.g., a client scratched themselves because their nails were not trimmed when needed, client developed multiple cavities, etc.).
Record log that ensures temps were checked daily.
